pages.title: The Memoir
pages.desc: A Life, Gathered — assembled a fragment at a time.
pages.body:
I am writing a memoir. Its name is A Life, Gathered, and that title is also its method: it comes together a fragment at a time, the way hardened crumbs of speedway mud used to turn up in coat pockets all week after a night at the track.

The book braids a present-tense frame against the chronological past, held together by a spine I think of as The Hands — what they built, played, broke and mended. It is dry at baseline, with dark humour where the weight needs a counterbalance, and restraint used as texture rather than reticence.

There is an excerpt if you would like to read a little.

pages.title: SCREAM
pages.desc: My open-source sprintcar speedway game — an homage to Premier.
pages.body:
I had this idea at thirteen or fourteen: to make a speedway game. I had been going to Premier Speedway for as long as I could remember — almost every season, from about six months old. My brother and I would spend the whole day getting so excited we invented our own version of fasting so we could fit as many Premier doughnuts into our tiny bellies as possible.

SCREAM is my homage to that place, built with the skills I have gathered over almost half a century so that it could be appreciated, shared, and given away for free — and I do mean free as in libre, not gratis.

“Free software” means software that respects users’ freedom and community — the freedom to run, copy, distribute, study, change and improve it. Think of “free” as in free speech, not free beer.— after the Free Software Foundation
▶SCREAMClick to load & play
Loads a live build in your browser. If it stalls, use the itch.io link below.

pages.title: The Pitstop
pages.desc: A fan-kept countdown and hub for the Grand Annual Sprintcar Classic.
pages.body:
The Pitstop is a fan-kept race-week hub and countdown for the Grand Annual Sprintcar Classic at Premier Speedway, Warrnambool — the schedule, the card, where to watch, and everything moving on the wire. Same maker, different livery: where this page is a manuscript, that one is an arcade sprintcar HUD.

It is not official, and it always defers to the club for the last word — but it is the fan’s pit board.

pages.title: The Reckoning Desk
pages.desc: A survivor-kept record of the Christian Brothers insolvency & scheme.
pages.body:
The Reckoning Desk is a survivor-kept public monitoring page documenting the Christian Brothers (Oceania Province) insolvency and the proposed Creditors’ Scheme of Arrangement. It gathers publicly available information, links to primary sources, and tracks the key dates.

It is kept carefully and plainly. It is not legal advice, and it defers questions of law to the proper places.

pages.title: The Music
pages.desc: Years of demos, originals, covers and ukulele — gathered up.
pages.body:
I have played for a long time, across a lot of instruments, and much of it never left the room I was raised in. Some of it has now. Here is a gathering of demos, originals, covers and a little ukulele.

pages.title: The Gallery
pages.desc: Sprintcars, mud and light — a reel of shots from the speedway.
pages.body:
Years of nights at Premier, camera in hand — sprintcars, mud, dust and light. Here is a reel of them, gathered from the SCREAM photography album.

pages.title: On the Name
pages.desc: Mahda — warrior hound, noble bear, luminous cradle.
pages.body:
Mahda (/ˈmɑː.də/) — n. prop. A personal name of composite Western and Eastern origin, signifying a sovereign thinker, a primal warrior spirit, and a focused mind.

It is a phonetic bridge: the Old Irish roots Mada / Madadh — the sacred “warrior hound” of tribal loyalty and relentless protection — and Matha, “bear” or “noble fighter”, the Celtic marker of strength and leadership; joined with the ancient Persian Mahda, meaning “luminous”, “cradle” or “moon-like”.

It is the primary title in the triad Mahda Christopher Greene — an anchor of personal authority balancing human history against grounded, earthly reality.
